item.title: Built in Fort Worth: Wistron Opens Advanced Manufacturing Plant to Produce NVIDIA AI Systems
item.description: The AI era runs on AI infrastructure. Many of these advanced systems are built and tested in Texas. Wistron opened its first U.S. manufacturing facility today in Fort Worth — a 324,000-square-foot greenfield plant producing superchips at the heart of some of the world’s most capable AI systems. In front of an audience of Wistron […]

item.title: NVIDIA Vera Rubin Driving Performance Per Watt, Lowest Token Cost for Partners Worldwide
item.description: Backed by 300 global partners, Vera Rubin is ramping up worldwide. NVIDIA partners CoreWeave, Google Cloud, Microsoft Azure and Mistral are among many deploying Vera Rubin, which delivers benchmark leadership on performance per watt and lowest token costs.

item.title: Built for Vera Rubin, NVIDIA Spectrum-6 Arrives in Gigascale AI Factories
item.description: Next-generation NVIDIA Spectrum-X Ethernet connects hundreds of thousands of GPUs with the performance, resilience and efficiency needed for gigascale AI, with AI infrastructure leaders already adopting.

item.title: At SIGGRAPH, NVIDIA Advances Graphics and Simulation With Agentic and Physical AI
item.description: From open models to real-time simulation, AI and graphics breakthroughs are transforming media, content creation and robotics.

item.title: NVIDIA Agent Toolkit Expands With New Omniverse Libraries, Putting AI Agents to Work Building Simulation-Ready Worlds
item.description: NVIDIA today announced that NVIDIA Agent Toolkit now includes NVIDIA Omniverse™ libraries — a collection of software components that give AI agents tools and skills to add physical AI capabilities to existing applications and prepare 3D content for simulation.

item.title: Japan Government, Industrial Leaders and NVIDIA Launch the World’s First National AI Infrastructure
item.description: NVIDIA today announced it is working with Noetra Corp. to launch an NVIDIA Vera Rubin AI factory with 13,750 NVIDIA Vera CPUs and 27,500 NVIDIA Rubin GPUs for national physical AI.

item.title: Japan’s Robotics and Manufacturing Leaders Build on NVIDIA Cosmos to Advance Physical AI Frontier
item.description: NVIDIA today announced that Japan’s physical AI leaders are building on the NVIDIA Cosmos™, NVIDIA Isaac™, NVIDIA Metropolis and NVIDIA Jetson™ platforms to accelerate the deployment of intelligent machines across manufacturing, mobility, infrastructure and robotics.

item.description: From open models to real-time simulation, AI and graphics breakthroughs are transforming media, content creation and robotics....

item.title: AI Infrastructure Archives
item.description: Home to leading manufacturers, robotics pioneers, infrastructure builders and iconic gaming companies, of course, Japan is one of the world’s centers of AI — building...

item.title: Networking Archives
item.description: AI has entered the gigascale era. The world’s most advanced AI factories are bringing together hundreds of thousands of GPUs and CPUs to train frontier...

item.title: Supercomputing Archives
item.description: Erin Davis calls it the “SuperDuperPOD.” That’s two things in one name: pharmaceutical giant Bristol Myers Squibb (BMS) already runs one of the largest AI...
